title: Force JSON output for routing and scoring helper calls
source: Anthropic docs: Structured outputs / increasing output consistency — https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/tool-use
description: The haiku-backed routing and scoring helpers (delegate.py:712, 787, 946, 1651) prompt the model and then parse its free-text reply for a decision. Free-text parsing of routing/scoring output is brittle and a single malformed reply can misroute a ticket or skew a score. Constraining these helper calls to emit strict JSON (prefilled assistant turn or stream-json with a schema-style instruction) makes downstream parsing deterministic.
proposed_changes: For the routing_prompt and scoring_prompt subprocess calls, append an explicit JSON-only contract (e.g. 'Respond with ONLY this JSON: {"choice": ..., "confidence": ...}, no prose') and parse with a strict json.loads guarded by a single retry on JSONDecodeError. Where the installed CLI supports it, run these helpers with --output-format json and read the structured result field rather than raw stdout. Centralize this into a _call_helper_json(prompt, model) utility so all four call sites share the same validated path.
